sources.name: Antiwar.com
sources.notes: Anti-imperial editorial line. Reliable on US foreign policy critique. NAVIGATION: fetch antiwar.com homepage; extract links whose href starts with 'https://news.antiwar.com/' — these are the aggregated news stories. Ignore Viewpoints, Blog, Radio, and Spotlight links. The 'News' section (labeled with an update timestamp) is the left-center column. rss.xml redirects to original.antiwar.com/feed/ (editorial/opinion only) — do NOT use RSS.

sources.name: The Grayzone
sources.notes: Investigative anti-establishment. Strong on document-based reporting; framing is opinionated. Covers Palestine solidarity crackdowns, media censorship around the war, Western lawfare against protest groups — all in scope.

sources.name: Al Jazeera English
sources.notes: Qatar-funded; editorially sympathetic to Palestinian cause. Strong on-the-ground Gaza reporting and ICC/ICJ proceedings. Good first-report source; note pro-Palestinian framing when writing context.

sources.name: +972 Magazine
sources.notes: Israeli-Palestinian independent outlet. Exceptional on settler violence, occupation policy, judicial reform, and IDF conduct inside the West Bank. Explicitly progressive framing is part of the record, not a disqualifier.

sources.name: Middle East Eye
sources.notes: UK-based independent. Strong sourcing inside Gaza and the West Bank. Good on ICC/ICJ proceedings and diplomatic back-channels.

sources.name: The Electronic Intifada
sources.notes: Palestinian-focused independent journalism. Good on BDS, legal proceedings, cultural and activist dimensions of the conflict. Strong pro-Palestinian editorial line.

sources.name: Google News — Israel/Palestine Search
sources.notes: Google News aggregation RSS — no API key needed. Pulls from hundreds of outlets. Trust tier 3: evaluate each linked article's source independently before writing it up. Use as a discovery net to catch stories the registered sources missed. Google wraps links in redirect URLs — extract the real article URL from each item's <link> tag.
